description: The semantic token references tok_lisraya_alignment_center, but that primitive token is absent. The required centered alignment for the main callout is therefore unresolved.
tok_lisraya_main_callout_rule references nonexistent tok_lisraya_border_callout_rule. This leaves the required Gold horizontal-rule treatment unresolved.
The source defines a 600px email body width, not a line height. This token assigns 600px to a line-height key and duplicates the actual width token.
Sunshine #faa31b is the gradient-callout section fallback background, not a button color. The specified button colors are Deep Blue fill, White label, and Gold arrow.
The required Gradient_callout.png asset has a mandated, do-not-rehost URL, but the catalog asset has no name or source URI recorded.

payload.name: Gradient callout background
payload.usage: Gradient Callout CTA Section background; do not re-host or recolor

description: The approved three-stop gradient is Golden Sand → Gold at 75% → Sunshine. The catalog records only Golden Sand and Sunshine, mislabels Sunshine as color_2, and omits the required middle Gold stop.
Links are conditional on background: Brand Blue underlined on light backgrounds and White underlined on dark backgrounds. The catalog supplies only an unconditional Brand Blue link color.
Body text is Graphite on light backgrounds but White on dark backgrounds. The catalog exposes only the Graphite default and omits the required dark-background White variant.

“Light” is not a weight specified for callout surfaces; the source specifies Light Blue at 70% tint and Golden Sand at 30% tint. A color/tint instruction has been incorrectly represented as a weight token.
No catalog entity captures the mandatory LISRAYA all-caps spelling, lowercase parenthesized generic name, or the conditional first-prominent external-use trademark rule.

default.registered_mark_condition: Only when generic name is included; first or most prominent mention only; external-facing documents only

description: This rule combines three independently applicable chart constraints: Brand Blue/Sky Blue dominance, Gold-only key-figure emphasis, and gray-for-placebo usage. Gold reservation is a hard exclusive restriction and should not be merged with the positive chart-palette preference.

rules.requirement: Use Brand Blue and Sky Blue as the dominant colors.
Reserve Sunshine/Gold exclusively for emphasizing key figures.
Use gray for placebo.

description: The source contains no table. It requires application of the primary > secondary > tertiary ratio shown in the guide, making the separate 'table binding' rule unsupported and duplicative of the palette-hierarchy rule.

payload.requirement: Apply the primary > secondary > tertiary ratio shown in the guide: blues and golds dominate, while tertiary colors are limited to accents and surfaces.

description: The source says 'Apply' the prescribed hierarchy, which is mandatory. Recording this as a strong default understates its hardness.

Mandatory, context-specific application rules for headings, body copy, callout surfaces, and links are absent from the candidate rules. These cannot be safely subsumed under the generic palette hierarchy because they specify exact colors, tints, treatments, and light/dark background conditions.

rules.requirement: Use Brand Blue, or White on dark or gradient backgrounds.
Use Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds and White on dark backgrounds; use increased line height on dark backgrounds.
Use Light Blue at 70% tint for data callouts and Golden Sand at 30% tint for text-heavy callouts.
Use underlined Brand Blue #00529b on light backgrounds and underlined White on dark backgrounds.

description: The Coral instruction contains materially different constraints: a hard prohibition for primary CTAs, headlines, and data; a positive sparing-tertiary-accent role; and a conditional email exception. None is represented by the candidates.

rules.requirement: Never use Coral.
Use Coral only as a sparing tertiary accent.

rules.exception: unless the brief calls for Coral
rules.requirement: Avoid Coral.

description: The universal logo-mark transparency requirement is missing. Its all-instances scope explicitly covers both logos and mark-as-pattern/graphic usage and must remain a hard constraint.

payload.requirement: Maintain the logo icon at 70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a.
payload.scope: all logo instances, including logo use and mark-as-pattern/graphic use

description: The assembly conflates five independently testable constraints with different targets and value types: campaign-headline tint, two named callout-box tints, wave-layer transparency, and a global prohibition. They should be atomic rules under the Tint Rules grouping, rather than one table-binding rule.

rules.requirement: Use approximately 40% tint of the applicable headline color.
rules.scope: campaign headlines labeled D and M; Gold or Brand Blue colorways

rules.requirement: Use a 70% tint.
Use a 30% tint.
Use 70% transparency.
Do not use ad-hoc tints; only palette values and the explicitly specified tint exceptions are permitted.

description: The source establishes a closed governance rule: ad-hoc tints are prohibited, with only two allowed categories—palette values and explicitly specified tints. The candidate's polarity=None and strong_default hardness fail to represent this as a hard negative constraint with explicit exceptions.

allowed_values: explicitly specified tints in this Tint Rules section

description: The single table-binding assembly conflates three independently governable requirements: the closed approved-gradient inventory, masthead/banner orientation, and the logo-mark-specific masthead gradient/mark placement. The latter two are not table membership facts and need separate rules with explicit scope and relations.

rules.scope: All LISRAYA gradient creation and use
rules.statement: Do not invent gradients; only the six listed approved gradient specifications may be used.
Run gradients horizontally, from dark/saturated at left to light at right, as shown.

rules.scope: Masthead applications using the LISRAYA logo mark
rules.statement: Use the Sunshine-to-light-gold gradient for contrast and place the logo mark at the lightest end.

description: The candidate does not encode the closed-set governance expressed by “never invent gradients” and “Approved values only”: governed action is selecting/creating gradients, the permitted set is the six table entries, and unlisted gradients are prohibited across background, wave-graphic, and logo-mark use.

governance.governed_action: create, select, or apply a gradient
governance.permitted_set: Brand Blue→Dark Navy; Sky Blue→Deep Blue; Sunshine→75% Gold; Golden Sand→White→Light Blue; Golden Sand→75% Gold→Sunshine; Sunshine→light gold
governance.prohibited_condition: Any gradient not in the approved set

payload.scope: Gradient creation or use as backgrounds, with the wave graphic, or with the LISRAYA logo mark

description: Hardness and polarity are incorrect/incomplete. “Never invent,” “Approved values only,” the required masthead gradient, logo placement, and horizontal direction are mandatory constraints, not a strong default with null polarity. The masthead logo rule also depends on the specific approved Sunshine-to-light-gold entry.

relations.target_constraint: horizontal; dark/saturated left to light right

description: The candidate conflates three independent constraints: mandatory contrast testing with WCAG thresholds, allowed/prohibited text-background pair guidance, and the prohibition on conveying meaning through color alone. A single must_not color-pair rule loses the positive requirements and distinct scopes.

rules.requirement: Test every text/background pair using WebAIM or an equivalent contrast checker; meet WCAG AA minimum contrast of 4.5:1 for body text and 3:1 for large text (at least 24px, or 19px bold).

Use the known-safe listed dark text colors on White, Light Blue, or Golden Sand, and White on Brand Blue, Deep Blue, or Dark Navy. Do not use Gold or Sunshine text on White; reserve golds for surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis.
Do not rely on color alone to convey meaning; pair color with icons, depth, clear copy, or visual feedback.

description: The candidate does not preserve the compliance governance in the source: verification is required for every text/background pair, WebAIM or an equivalent checker is the accepted verification mechanism, and WCAG AA thresholds vary by text size/weight.

The candidate's must_not polarity is incomplete and misleading: the source includes affirmative must requirements to test contrast and provide non-color meaning cues, plus positive known-safe pair guidance. Its scope is also too narrow because color-alone meaning applies beyond text-color pairs.

payload.polarity_correction: Retain must_not only for Gold/Sunshine text on White and color-alone meaning; represent contrast testing and supplementary cues as must rules.

description: One assembly rule conflates the section layout, background asset/fallback and narrow skip exception, headline and button styling, and the independently immutable asymmetric button-radius requirement. These have different scopes and conditional behavior and must be separately governable.

prohibitions: Do not simplify to a single radius value.
split_rules.scope: Every Gradient Callout CTA button instance
Gradient Callout CTA section background image

description: The background-image exception requires explicit governance: it is permitted only for enumerated very rare failure/fallback cases, permits removal of only four background-image attributes, and obligates retention of the Sunshine fallback and every other visual rule. The candidate's single strong-default assembly does not encode this conditional permission, bounded modification set, or retention obligations.

prohibited_changes: skip the image for ordinary rendering
payload.scope: Gradient Callout CTA section background image

description: hardness=strong_default and polarity=None understate explicit hard and prohibitive source language. The background asset must not be re-hosted or recolored, and the four-value radius is mandatory on every instance, including fallback instances; simplification, corner swapping, and decimal rounding are prohibited.

prohibitions: Do not re-host the gradient PNG.
Do not recolor the gradient PNG.
Do not simplify, reorder, or round the required four-value button radius.

relations.if: background image is skipped under the governed exception
relations.then: the exact button-radius rule remains required

description: The Outlook fallback is conditional governance, not an unscoped strong default: Outlook desktop/Word-engine clients ignore absolute positioning; pixel-perfect all-client delivery has approved pre-composite/background-image alternatives; when those are infeasible, the live implementation may intentionally degrade only in Outlook while retaining the other component requirements.

approved_options: Pre-composite the badge, shadow, accent radii, and icon as a transparent PNG.
Bake the badge-on-edge treatment into the box background image while keeping text live.
If neither is feasible, ship the live version and accept the specified Outlook-only degradation.
payload.degradation: Badge may render flush at the inner-left/top-left in normal flow and the box may not retain the intended inset in Outlook desktop.
invariant_requirements: Brand Blue Nunito Sans weight-800 text
payload.trigger: When absolute positioning is unsupported or pixel-perfect fidelity across all email clients is required.

description: This rule has no polarity even though the palette table binds specific colors to specific icon-callout parts. Its scope must be limited to the icon callout, rather than treated as a generic palette rule.

The candidate combines independently scoped requirements and opposing polarities: conditional first/prominent external-use of ® is a positive requirement, while omission on subsequent mentions and outside the external/with-generic condition is prohibitive. The lowercase parenthetical generic-name format applies whenever the generic name is used, not only to the first trademarked mention.

rules.rule_text: Use LISRAYA® (brepocitinib) on the first or most prominent qualifying mention.
rules.scope: external-facing documents; first or most prominent mention; only when the generic name is included

rules.rule_text: Do not repeat the ® symbol after the first/most prominent mention, and do not use it except in external-facing documents when the generic name is included.
Write the generic name as lowercase text in parentheses: (brepocitinib).
rules.scope: whenever the generic name is written

description: No candidate rule captures the all-caps LISRAYA requirement for all written materials or its explicit supersession of the February 2026 general-guide title-case rule. This is a hard global copy rule with a precedence relation, not merely a first-mention trademark condition.

payload.rule_text: Always write LISRAYA in all caps.

description: The two candidate rules have the same table-binding scope and appear duplicative. The table is one typography assignment matrix, not two independent assembly rules.

payload.scope: Typography hierarchy by content level and delivery medium

description: A generic table-binding assembly does not preserve the table's conditional medium/content-level assignments, the Agenda campaign-headline-only exception, or the allowed weight inventories. These are distinct scoped requirements rather than undifferentiated typography metadata.

split_rules.requirements: Use Agenda for print headlines/headers; use Nunito Sans for digital/email headlines, body, and footers; use Aptos for Word/PPT/Excel fallback; use Nunito Sans for print body and footers.
split_rules.scope: Print, digital/email, and Word/PPT/Excel fallback typography by headlines, body copy, and footers/fine print

split_rules.requirements: Agenda may be used as live text only for the campaign headline; digital headlines otherwise use Nunito Sans in bolder weights and Title Case.
Limit Agenda to Light, Regular, Medium, Semibold, Bold; Nunito Sans to Light, Regular, SemiBold, Bold, ExtraBold; and Aptos to Regular or Bold.

description: The email font requirement has operational governance that is not represented by the table-binding rules: a prescribed CSS stack, a required webfont-loading action where supported, and a client-capability fallback rationale for clients that strip webfonts.

requirements.fallback_governance: Retain the complete stack so Outlook and other clients that strip webfonts fall back gracefully.
requirements.implementation: Load Nunito Sans via @font-face or a Google Fonts link when the email client supports webfonts.

description: The mandatory email accessibility limits and their exceptions/ownership are absent from the table assembly: 16px is mandated for primary body, running text may never be below 12px, body line-height has a 150% minimum, dark-background light text requires 160%, and ISI sizing is owned by a locked component.

requirements.body_line_height_minimum: Use at least 150% line height for all body copy.
requirements.dark_background_condition: For light text on dark backgrounds, use 160% line height, for example 16px/26px.
requirements.ownership_exception: ISI sizing is controlled by the locked component and is not overridden by this rule.
requirements.primary_body_minimum: Primary body copy must be at least 16px.

description: The specified email type scale and hierarchy prohibitions are missing as enforceable rules. In particular, secondary text is conditional and may not be primary content, footnotes require visual separation, and adjacent blocks may not use identical size and weight to indicate different hierarchy levels.

requirements.footnote_constraint: Footnote/reference text must be noticeably smaller than primary data and clearly separated from it.
requirements.hierarchy_constraint: Use a clear visual reading hierarchy; never use adjacent text blocks with the same size and weight to signal different hierarchy levels.
requirements.supporting_body_constraint: Use supporting body sparingly and never for primary content.

payload.scope: Email H1, H2, H3, body, supporting body, and footnote text

description: The group retains only the campaign-specific ALL-CAPS exception and omits the general headline/heading title-case rule. This loses the default casing policy that the campaign and chart rules qualify.

payload.scope: headlines and headings, except explicitly defined ALL-CAPS headline variants
payload.statement: Use initial caps (title case): capitalize nouns, pronouns, adjectives, verbs, and adverbs; lowercase articles, conjunctions, and prepositions.

description: The chart/infographic headline exception is missing. It requires both ALL CAPS and channel-specific typeface treatment, and it overrides the general headline title-case default.

payload.statement: Set chart and infographic headlines in ALL CAPS; use Agenda for print and Nunito Sans Bold or heavier for email fallback.

description: The body-copy sentence-case requirement is absent from the candidate group.

payload.statement: Use sentence case for body copy.

description: The candidate campaign rule does not preserve the related general prohibition: ALL CAPS is forbidden in body copy, buttons, and subheads except for the explicitly listed exceptions. The prohibition has a distinct negative polarity and scoped exception relation.

payload.statement: Do not use ALL CAPS in body copy, buttons, or subheads, except in explicitly specified cases above.

description: No extracted rule captures the general bolding policy: bold serves hierarchy/key takeaways rather than decoration, with a per-section limit on headings, one key phrase or claim per paragraph, and the statistical callout figure.

payload.rule_text: Use bold for hierarchy and key takeaways, not decoration. Per email section, bold only headings, at most one key phrase or claim per paragraph, and the statistical callout figure.

description: No rule captures the mandatory bolding cases and their conditions, including the first-instance-per-section limit for a focal brand name in a benefit claim.

payload.rule_text: Always bold callout text specified by the callout specs, statistical callout numbers, and the brand name when it is the focal point of a benefit-claim sentence; bold that brand-name instance only once per section.
payload.scope: email sections; first brand-name instance per section

description: No prohibition rule preserves the explicit never-bold list, including the quantitative approximately-15% cap on running body text within each section.

payload.rule_text: Never bold entire paragraphs, footnotes or references, prepositional filler, or the generic name. Do not bold more than approximately 15% of running body text in any section.
payload.scope: any email section; running body text

description: No rule captures the emphasis-device combination restriction, its two-device maximum, and the narrow chart-headline/campaign-lockup exception.

payload.rule_text: Do not combine bold, ALL CAPS, and color emphasis on the same string; use at most two emphasis devices per element. The triple combination is allowed only in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup.

description: The source contains two independent formatting constraints—underlining and italic use—that should be represented as separate rules so their distinct scopes and exceptions are not conflated or omitted.

rules.rule_text: Do not use underlining except for real hyperlinks.
Do not use italics for emphasis in patient copy. Reserve italics for study names, notes such as "Note:", and citations.

description: The source is only an editorial/style heading and contains no table requirement, binding instruction, or assembly rule.
The abbreviation rule contains a distinct running-copy requirement that is not represented: after the initial full-form mention, running copy must use DM to avoid repetition.

payload.requirement: After the first mention of dermatomyositis, use the abbreviation DM in running copy.

description: The categorical prohibition on DM in headlines, headings, and subheads is missing. It has a different scope and polarity from the running-copy abbreviation requirement and should be a separate rule.

payload.requirement: Do not use DM in headlines, headings, or subheads; always use the full word dermatomyositis.

description: The standalone-section rule needs the explicit governance exception: the section-level first-mention requirement is waived only when the assembly brief establishes that DM was introduced upstream.

payload.exception: Do not repeat the establishment only when the assembly brief explicitly states that the abbreviation is established upstream.
payload.requirement: Each standalone section must establish dermatomyositis (DM) at its own first mention.

description: Patient-material terminology is absent, including its audience-specific exception: patient materials must use person-first wording rather than patients, while patients is permitted in HCP materials.

payload.exception: The term patients is acceptable in HCP materials.
payload.requirement: Use person-first terminology such as someone with dermatomyositis or people with dermatomyositis; do not use patients.

description: The required care team terminology is missing: staff must not be used for the network of patients, HCPs, and support systems.

payload.requirement: Use care team, not staff.
payload.scope: references to the network of patients, HCPs, and support systems

description: The single assembly rule conflates four separable concerns: the approved callout-style inventory, a same-style competition prohibition, and three distinct style specifications with different visual and responsive requirements. These should be independently governable rules rather than one table-binding assembly.

children.rule: Use only the three approved callout styles: main, secondary/data, and text-heavy.
children.title: Approved callout styles

children.rule: Never place two callouts of the same approved style in competition within one screen or section.
children.title: Callout style competition prohibition
Text-heavy callout specification and mobile collapse

description: The source contains an explicit absolute governance constraint that is not captured by the candidate identity: prohibition polarity ("never"), the governed object (same callout style), and scope (within one screen or section). It also establishes a closed approved set of three styles.

payload.rule: Never use two callouts of the same approved style in competition on one screen or section.
payload.scope: within a single screen or section
payload.title: Same-style callout competition prohibition

description: The candidate's single strong_default hardness cannot preserve the mixed source modalities: "never" is a hard prohibition; "may include one line icon" is permission; "use judiciously" is discretionary guidance; bracketed GENERAL measurements are defaults; and the text-heavy two-column layout has a mandatory mobile single-column behavior. Polarity is also unset despite both requirements and a prohibition being present.

One rule conflates independently selectable messaging modules: headline, benefit copy and benefit bullets, two MOA variants, administration copy, CTA, and support-program naming. The primary and alternate MOA entries are specifically alternatives, while the other entries are distinct placements rather than a single all-or-nothing copy requirement.

split_into.topic: approved product-benefit copy and associated bullets
approved support-program name and service description

description: The source labels this as an approved, verbatim framework, but the candidate's generic hard/must representation does not retain the necessary governance facets: approved/locked-copy status, verbatim-use requirement, applicability only when the corresponding module is used, and the mutually exclusive primary-versus-alt MOA selection.

governance.applicability: when using the applicable campaign-message module; not a requirement to include every module

description: A single polarity of must incorrectly implies that both MOA statements must appear together. Source labels one as primary and the other as alt; they must be represented as alternative approved copy options, not cumulative mandatory content.

payload.constraint: If MOA copy is used, use exactly one approved MOA variant verbatim.

description: The single hard/must core-principles rule conflates three distinct voice requirements with a separate context-flexibility/invariance rule. In particular, unit 0004 contains both a permission (tone may flex by context) and an absolute invariant (core voice never changes), which cannot be represented faithfully by one unconditional must rule.

new_rules.requirement: State first-of-its-kind significance clearly and directly; do not overstate or sensationalize it.
Use genuine empathy about diabetes mellitus's daily impact and keep patients at the center, without becoming overly emotional.
Use plain, respectful, accurate language that is easy to understand.
Allow slight tone adaptation by context, but never change the core brand voice.

description: This rule combines two independently testable obligations with different domains: required patient-quote attribution/styling and required pairing with warm lifestyle imagery. Its imagery class also obscures the copy/editorial attribution requirement.

rules.requirement: Attribute each patient quote as '- [Name], LISRAYA patient' and render it in italic or otherwise distinguished styling.
Pair patient quotes with warm lifestyle imagery.

description: The source places this under 'Required Elements Within Sections' and states mandatory quote treatment and imagery pairing. Modeling it as a strong default weakens a mandatory requirement.

This row conflates independent canvas sizing, responsive behavior, advisory grid guidance, section inset/bleed behavior, and vertical-spacing rules. These have different scopes and hardness; in particular, the 12-column item is a mental model while the fixed width, inset, and zero-gap requirements are mandatory constraints.

The rule is unpolarized and only a strong default despite explicit hard language in its source: the canvas is fixed/exactly 600px, text must retain 24px insets, all spacing must use 8px multiples, and external section margins are prohibited. Its table-binding scope is also unsupported: no source unit mentions tables.

payload.note: Apply this hardness only to the mandatory canvas/inset/rhythm descendants after the split; retain the grid mental-model item as guidance.

description: The existing CTA rule captures only the primary-CTA maximum. It misses the mandatory shared CTA component specification, including the ≥48px accessibility touch target, and the relation that the secondary CTA keeps the same metrics while changing only its color/border treatment.
No rule captures the constrained wave system: exactly two named colorways, each available in long and short variants. This is a distinct specification rule, not merely a color-approval constraint.

payload.rule_text: Use fluid wave forms only in the Sunshine/Gold and Sky Blue/Deep Blue colorways, with long and short variants for each colorway.

description: The mandatory layered-wave construction is absent: the top layer must be 70% transparent over its specified base color, and the translucent layered quality must always be preserved.

payload.rule_text: Construct waves with a 70% transparent top layer over the base wave: Gold over Sunshine or Sky Blue over Deep Blue. Always preserve the layered, translucent quality.

description: The explicit compliance constraint "Approved color values only" is not represented as a governed rule. It needs a mandatory hardness, an applicability scope limited to waves, and an approved-values restriction; permitted placement/use should not weaken this restriction.

payload.rule_text: Use approved color values only for wave graphics.

description: The permitted wave-placement and purpose guidance is absent and should remain separate from the mandatory color-approval rule: waves may be used alone or over photography/gradient backgrounds to support flow, frame content, or add depth.

payload.rule_text: Waves may be used alone or overlaid on photography or gradient backgrounds to support layout flow, frame content, or add depth.

description: The masthead-approved-device rule is absent. It has governed applicability (masthead copy positions), a prescribed gradient direction and CMYK endpoint, and a required logo-mark placement at the lightest end.

payload.rule_text: For masthead copy positions, use the approved masthead gradient with logo mark: Sunshine to 0C,10M,78Y,0K, with the logo mark placed at the lightest end.

description: The required photography treatment is not represented: imagery must depict warm, realistic patient lifestyle moments and communicate return to everyday life.

payload.statement: Use warm, realistic patient lifestyle photography showing simple everyday activities and conveying a return to everyday life and getting back to oneself.

description: The mandatory image-container treatment is missing. Images must use the Accent shape rather than plain rectangular or circular crops.

payload.statement: House images in the Accent shape: asymmetric rounded-corner crops. Do not use plain rectangular or circular crops.

description: The hard accessibility requirement needs explicit universal scope: the source applies it to every image, not only selected photography or campaign assets.

payload.statement: Every image requires descriptive alt text.

description: The candidate is misframed as a table-binding assembly. The source defines a general asymmetric Brand Accent Shape, channel-specific approved radius values and email orientation behavior, plus a separate charts/tables exception. Tables are not a binding target; they are explicitly excluded from the asymmetric shape. These need distinct rules with an explicit exception relation rather than one table-oriented cluster.

rules.rule: Use the Brand Accent Shape: a rectangle with selectively enlarged opposite-corner radii and slight radii on the remaining two corners. Approved print pairs are 1.25in/0.0625in for large containers and photos, 0.5in/0.0625in for banners, and 0.4375in/0.0625in for small callout boxes.
rules.scope: general containers, callout text, and photography

rules.rule: Use 120px/6px for large, 48px/6px for banners, and 42px/6px for callouts. Apply the large radius to one diagonal pair and the 6px radius to the other; keep the same diagonal orientation across all email sections. Default orientation is large radii at top-left and bottom-right.
Do not use the asymmetric Brand Accent Shape on charts or tables; use a uniform 12px radius on all four corners.

description: The candidate's displayed polarity of None and single strong_default hardness do not preserve source semantics: email orientation consistency is mandatory with a default orientation, while the chart/table rule is a hard negative exception. Email scope (96dpi) and the print-versus-email distinction are also material constraints.

payload.note: Replace the unspecific assembly with scoped positive, defaulted, and prohibitive exception rules as defined in the split patch; retain explicit print and email/96dpi scopes and the exception relation.

description: The container specification and the rounded-bar/icon treatment are independent requirements from separate source units. A chart can comply with one while violating the other, so they should not be governed by one combined rule.

child_rules.requirements: Use uniform rounded corners, a Light Blue (#E6F0F9) panel, and a rounded Brand Blue header bar; maintain a clean, easy-to-scan system-consistent presentation.
Use rounded, approachable shapes for bars and chart icons, including rounded bar tops.

description: Statistical-callout hierarchy and footnote treatment are separately testable typography requirements. Combining them makes a violation of callout emphasis indistinguishable from a violation of footnote separation.

child_rules.requirements: Make statistical callouts large and bold, with the percentage figure as the hero and supporting copy in a lighter weight beneath.
Set footnotes noticeably smaller and clearly separate them from primary data.

description: The chart headline rule is unrepresented: it specifies channel-dependent typeface, all-caps treatment, Brand Blue color, and a thin Gold rule beneath. None of the candidate rules captures this requirement.

payload.requirements: Set chart headlines in Agenda for print and Nunito Sans Bold+ for email; use ALL CAPS in Brand Blue with a thin Gold rule beneath.

description: The candidate conflates two independently scoped hard constraints: mandatory minimal-line icon styling for new icons and palette-only restrictions for icon colors/backgrounds. They should be separate rules so their distinct applicability and requirements are preserved.

rules.requirement: New icons must use minimal line illustration and match the established styling: consistent stroke weight, rounded line caps, and single-color line work.
Icon colors and backgrounds may be adjusted only within the brand palette.

description: The low-vision accessibility guidance is not represented by the candidate rule. Its source modality is advisory ('Consider'), not a hard must, and it includes email-specific minimum size plus a default color/background treatment.

payload.requirement: Consider scale and contrast for low-vision audiences. In email, use a minimum render size of 32×32px; default to Brand Blue strokes on light backgrounds.
payload.scope: icons for low-vision audiences; email rendering

description: The candidate conflates two separately enforceable requirements: a visual assembly pattern requiring alternating approved section backgrounds, and an explicit [GENERAL] governance requirement that section designers record the selected background in component metadata. The metadata declaration has a named accountable actor, required evidence location, and an assembly-enabling purpose that should be independently auditable.

rules.requirement: Use the approved alternating section-background sequence—White, Light Blue, Golden Sand, and gradient—so adjacent assembled sections do not use identical backgrounds or visually merge.

rules.evidence: Component metadata contains the section background-color declaration.
rules.purpose: Enables assemblers to sequence sections without placing two identical backgrounds adjacent.
rules.requirement: Section designers must declare their section's background color in the component metadata.
rules.scope: all section designers and component metadata

description: The explicit [GENERAL] compliance instruction requires governance facets beyond the visual outcome: accountable party (section designers), mandatory action (declare), governed artifact/field (section background color), evidence location (component metadata), and downstream consumer/purpose (assemblers prevent identical adjacent backgrounds). These should not be represented merely as part of an assembly styling rule.

governance.control_objective: allow sequencing that prevents two identical adjacent section backgrounds

description: The approved three-color palette, its mandatory hierarchy, and the role of each color are absent from candidate rules.

payload.requirement: Use only the approved brand colors in this hierarchy: Deep Blue #00529B as dominant, Amber Orange #FAA31B as secondary accent, and Golden Yellow #FFC60A as tertiary accent.

description: The mandatory headline/header typography rule for Agenda is absent from candidate rules.

payload.requirement: Use Agenda as the primary headline font for all headlines and headers.

description: The mandatory print body-copy typography rule for Nunito Sans is absent from candidate rules.

payload.requirement: Use Nunito Sans for all body copy in print.

description: The prescribed CTA shape and gradient treatment are absent. The CTA uses the supplied asymmetric rounded shape and a left-to-right Golden Yellow-to-Amber Orange gradient.

payload.requirement: Use the supplied CTA shape and treatment: an asymmetric rounded form with a horizontal gradient from #FFDF55 at the left to #FAA21B at the right.

description: No rule captures the brand-mandated minimum body-copy size of 16px; the preference for larger text should remain advisory rather than becoming a maximum or hard requirement.

payload.rule_text: Use body copy at 16px or larger; larger body text is preferred.

description: No rule captures the mandatory minimum 150% line height, including the stronger contextual guidance for light text on dark backgrounds.

payload.rule_text: Set line height to at least 150%; use more line height for light-on-dark text.

description: The required visual-hierarchy guidance to use a type scale is absent.

payload.rule_text: Use a type scale to establish visual hierarchy.

description: The contrast-testing and text-legibility requirements are absent; they should not be weakened into a mere suggestion.

payload.rule_text: Test contrast and proof all text for legibility.

description: The absolute prohibition against relying on color alone is absent and must retain its negative polarity.

payload.rule_text: Never rely on color alone to convey information.

description: The form-label requirement is absent. Its prohibitions on placeholder-only and disappearing labels must remain explicit.

payload.rule_text: Form labels must always remain visible; do not use placeholder text as the label and do not use disappearing labels.

description: The minimum 48px click/tap target requirement is absent.

payload.rule_text: Make click and tap targets at least 48px.

description: Animation guidance contains distinct general-motion requirements and email-only GIF constraints. They must not be represented as one undifferentiated rule because the email requirements have a narrower scope.

payload.rule_text: Use quick animation transitions that do not interrupt user flow, avoid excessive motion, and provide a way to pause effects.

description: The cross-device design requirement is absent from the animation/accessibility guidance.

payload.rule_text: Design for phone, tablet, and desktop.

description: The email-specific animated-GIF frame-one requirement is absent and must be scoped to general email rather than all animation.

payload.rationale: Outlook shows only the first GIF frame.
payload.rule_text: In general email, animated GIFs must communicate the key message in frame 1 because Outlook may display only the first frame.

description: The email-specific prohibition on autoplaying motion over roughly five seconds without a static fallback is absent; both the approximate threshold and fallback condition should be preserved.

payload.rule_text: In general email, do not use autoplaying motion longer than approximately 5 seconds unless a static fallback is provided.

description: The universal descriptive-alt-text requirement for images is absent.

payload.rule_text: Provide descriptive alt text on all images.

description: The requirement to make transcripts available for every video or audio asset is absent.

payload.rule_text: Make transcripts available for any video or audio.

description: The accessibility section is explicitly brand-mandated and names WCAG as its reference standard; no governance/reference relation is represented.

payload.rule_text: Treat accessibility requirements in this section as brand-mandated and use WCAG (wcag.com) as the reference standard.

description: Audience governance is absent: the guide applies to patient/DTC materials, audience must be confirmed in every brief, and terminology differs by audience (use “someone/people with dermatomyositis” for patient materials; “patients” is acceptable for HCP materials). This is distinct from the patient-brochure master-reference rule.

relations.detail: HCP materials permit “patients,” unlike patient/DTC materials.

payload.rule_text: Confirm the target audience in every brief before writing. This guide governs patient (DTC) materials: use “someone/people with dermatomyositis” in patient materials. “Patients” is acceptable in HCP materials.
payload.scope: patient (DTC) materials; terminology exception for HCP materials

description: The candidate combines six independently actionable conventions: section structure, required metadata, footnote assignment, locked-component adjacency, dark-section placement, and email-code compatibility. These have different subjects, actors, scopes, and enforcement conditions and must not be represented as one rule.

rules.requirement: Each section must be a self-contained 600px table-based block with its own 32px top and bottom padding and declared background color; no margins may exist outside the section, because sections are directly stacked.
Section metadata must declare background color, whether the section establishes dermatomyositis (DM), consumed footnote symbols, and CTA presence.
Footnote symbols must be superscript and assigned in order of appearance at assembly time; sections must use placeholder tokens rather than hard-coded daggers.
rules.scope: sections containing more than one referenced claim

rules.requirement: Adjacent sections must not include decorative elements whose visual continuity depends on the locked component above or below.
rules.scope: sections adjacent to locked header, footer, or ISI components

rules.requirement: A dark-background section must not be placed adjacent to another dark-background section.
Use HEX for all colors, px for all sizing, inline CSS only, and duplicate bgcolor attributes for Outlook.

description: The clustered rule loses explicit governance: metadata declarations enable assembly checks; footnote assignment is performed at assembly time; locked components are inserted by the core team; and dark-background adjacency is the assembler's responsibility. These actor and lifecycle facets are material compliance requirements.

footnote_assignment.method: assign superscript symbols in order of appearance

description: The semantic token references tok_lisraya_alignment_center, but no such primitive exists in the catalog. The required centered alignment for the main callout is therefore unresolved.
The main-callout semantic token references tok_lisraya_border_callout_rule, which does not exist. The required Gold horizontal rules cannot be resolved.
A 600px email canvas width is stored under a line_height key. Width and line-height are distinct primitives; this is a property/type mismatch and duplicates the correctly typed size token.

The primitive ID describes a Light Blue callout tint, its key describes logo Gold opacity, and it is also reused for wave transparency. Logo-mark transparency and wave-layer transparency are separate conditions even though both are 70%.
The 16px source value is padding between the Gold rules and the callout text, not a line-height. The token changes the property being specified.
No source statement assigns a font weight of Light to callout surfaces or secondary/data callouts. “Light Blue” is a color name, not a weight instruction.
The catalog provides only Brand Blue headline/body/link color semantics. It omits the required White variants for headings, body text, and underlined links on dark or gradient backgrounds.
Campaign-headline semantics capture the 40% tinted letters but omit the two approved headline colorways: Gold/Sunshine and Brand Blue, with the D/M tint derived from the same selected hue.

payload.tinted_letters_rule: Use 40% tint of the selected headline color.

description: The Gradient Callout background asset has only a display name; the required immutable source URL and the instruction not to re-host or recolor it are missing.
The fixed 248px CTA width is required for the Gradient Callout CTA but is absent from the primitive tokens; only its 64px height is captured.
The icon-callout badge size is cataloged, but the required 48px inner icon size is missing as a token.
The 50% value appears only in an illustrative clinical-copy sentence (“nearly 50% more improvement”), not as a reusable component/design token.

The source defines three distinct palette tiers, not one undifferentiated palette binding: Primary is explicitly the dominant tier, while Secondary and Tertiary are separately scoped color sets. The three generic table-binding rules should be partitioned and tier-qualified so that the primary/dominant relationship is retained.

This combines a chart palette default with a scoped, absolute reservation on Sunshine/Gold. “Strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures” is a prohibition/permission constraint and should not be bundled with the chart color-preference rule.

rules.requirement: Use Brand Blue and Sky Blue as the dominant colors.
Use Sunshine/Gold only to emphasize key figures; use gray for placebo.

description: The assembly/table-binding rule and palette-hierarchy rule appear to encode the same source requirement: application follows the primary > secondary > tertiary ratio shown in the guide. They should be one rule with the guide ratio retained as its reference/binding.

relation.reference: palette ratio shown in the guide

payload.requirement: Apply the palette in the primary > secondary > tertiary ratio shown in the guide: blues and golds dominate, while tertiary colors are limited to accents and surfaces.

description: No candidate rule covers the mandatory foreground-color requirements for headings and body text, including the light-versus-dark background condition and the required increased line height for white body text on dark backgrounds.

payload.requirement: Use Brand Blue for headings, except use White on dark or gradient backgrounds. Use Graphite #212121 for body text on light backgrounds and White on dark backgrounds; when White body text is used on dark backgrounds, increase line height per §1.2.

description: The specific callout-surface tint requirements are unrepresented: data callouts and text-heavy callouts have different mandated colors and tint percentages.

payload.requirement: Use Light Blue at 70% tint for data callouts and Golden Sand at 30% tint for text-heavy callouts.

description: Coral governance is absent. The source contains both absolute prohibited uses and a scoped email exception governed by an approved brief; these facets must be explicit rather than reduced to a generic tertiary-accent preference.

payload.requirement: Use Coral only as a sparing tertiary accent; never use it for primary CTAs, headlines, or data. Avoid Coral in email sections unless the brief explicitly calls for it.

description: The required link treatments are missing, including Brand Blue #00529b on light backgrounds, White on dark backgrounds, and underlining in both contexts.

payload.requirement: Use underlined Brand Blue #00529b links on light backgrounds and underlined White links on dark backgrounds.

description: The logo-mark transparency requirement is absent. This is an all-instances hard compliance constraint with an explicit identity value and two application contexts (logo and mark used as pattern/graphic).

payload.requirement: Maintain the logo icon at 70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a.
payload.scope: all logo-icon instances, including the mark used as a pattern or graphic

description: The single assembly conflates four independently scoped component specifications with a global exclusion rule. It also conflates tint values with the distinct wave-layer transparency requirement. These need atomic rules so their component scope, value type, and exception relation can be represented without ambiguity.

scope.applicability: all uses unless covered by an explicit specified tint

description: The absolute compliance statement “No other ad-hoc tints” is not represented with prohibition polarity, allowed alternatives, or its exceptions. Palette values are generally allowed, and the three explicit tint prescriptions are permitted exceptions; without these facets, compliant use cannot be determined.

A single rule-level polarity of None and hardness of strong_default cannot faithfully encode both the affirmative 70% transparency specification and the strict negative “No other” tint prohibition. The transparency directive is also outside the tint-only prohibition scope and must not be treated as a tint exception.

no_relation.reason: Transparency is not a tint and is not an exception to the tint prohibition.

description: The single assembly row conflates an approved-gradient inventory with independent mandatory constraints: gradients must be limited to approved values, masthead/banner gradients have a horizontal dark/saturated-to-light left-to-right direction, and the Sunshine-to-light-gold gradient is specifically required for logo-mark mastheads with the mark at the lightest end. These have different scopes, polarity, and applicability and should not remain one undifferentiated table binding.

rules.statement: Do not invent gradients; use approved gradient values only.
Run gradients horizontally, dark/saturated to light, from left to right as shown.

rules.scope: Masthead applications with the LISRAYA logo mark
rules.statement: Use the Sunshine-to-light-gold masthead gradient for contrast, and place the logo mark at the lightest end.
Approved gradients may be used as backgrounds, with the wave graphic, and with the LISRAYA logo mark.

description: The candidate conflates affirmative known-safe text/background combinations with a prohibition on Gold or Sunshine text on White. A single must_not rule cannot faithfully represent the positive approved-pair list and the separate negative exception/use restriction.

rules.statement: Use the known-safe pairs: Brand Blue, Deep Blue, Dark Navy, or Graphite text on White, Light Blue, or Golden Sand; and White text on Brand Blue, Deep Blue, or Dark Navy.
Do not use Gold or Sunshine text on White because it fails contrast. Restrict gold tones to surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis.

description: The source contains a mandatory contrast-verification control absent from the candidate: every text/background pair must be tested with WebAIM or an equivalent tool, against explicit WCAG AA thresholds. The verification method, universal scope, and measurable thresholds are required governance facets.

payload.statement: Test every text/background pair for WCAG AA contrast compliance.

description: The candidate omits the independent accessibility requirement that color must not be the sole carrier of meaning. This is not implied by contrast-safe color pairs and requires a separate rule with required redundant cues.

payload.statement: Do not rely on color alone to convey meaning; pair color with icons, depth, clear copy, or visual feedback.

description: The single assembly mixes independently enforceable section-background, headline, button-style, and immutable-radius requirements. It also combines a conditional background exception with unconditional button-radius requirements, so one rule-level hardness and polarity cannot faithfully represent the source.

rules.name: gradient_callout_button_signature_radius
prohibitions: Do not simplify to a single radius value.

description: The background-image exception needs explicit governance: omission is allowed only for plain-text/AMP fallback, clients stripping all images, or a hard size budget. On exception, only the four image attributes may be removed; Sunshine fallback, headline color, button color, and the exact asymmetric radius remain mandatory. Outlook image loss is a reason for the fallback color, not blanket permission to omit the image.

The candidate group has fallback and palette rows but no primary Icon Callout Box assembly rule. The source defines the component's intended proof-point use, required box/badge/text construction, 50/50 edge-overhang geometry, default 80px/48px sizing, and proportional scaling relationships.

requirements.badge_box_relation: Center the white icon badge exactly on the blue box's left edge, with 50% of the badge over the box and 50% over the page background.
requirements.container: Place the callout on the section/page background; inset the Light Blue box from the left by W/2 so the badge can overhang outward.
requirements.scaling: For a non-default badge size, preserve inset=W/2, box-left-padding=W/2+24px, and vertical badge offset=-H/2.
requirements.text: Use Brand Blue Nunito Sans weight 800 at approximately 21px/28px, left aligned and vertically centered; keep copy clear of the overhanging badge.

payload.scope: Icon Callout Box instances used for supporting proof-point statements

description: The mandatory corner treatment is not represented by either candidate. Governance is explicit: the box and badge radii, diagonal orientation, and 5:1 relationship are invariant; uniform radii, swapped corners, and diagonal flips are prohibited.

requirements.badge_border_radius: 20px 4px 20px 4px in TL TR BR BL order
requirements.box_border_radius: 40px 8px 40px 8px in TL TR BR BL order
prohibitions: Do not use a uniform radius.
Do not flip the large-corner diagonal.
requirements.shape_relation: Both shapes use a 5:1 ratio with large top-left and bottom-right corners and near-point top-right and bottom-left corners.

payload.scope: every Icon Callout Box instance, including badge

description: No candidate preserves the universal mandatory shadow requirement. The white badge must have a subtle Dark Navy low-opacity shadow on every instance; this is not merely a stylistic preference.

requirements.shadow: Apply a subtle drop shadow so the badge lifts from the Light Blue box.

payload.scope: every Icon Callout Box white icon badge

description: The Outlook material contains two differently governed cases and should not be one unconditional strong-default fallback: Outlook desktop's Word engine degrades absolute positioning, while pixel-perfect all-client delivery conditionally calls for pre-compositing/baking the treatment. When that is infeasible, the live version is allowed with an explicitly scoped Outlook-only degradation and retained visual invariants.

rules.condition: When pixel-perfect fidelity across all email clients is required
rules.requirement: Use a transparent pre-composited badge treatment or bake the badge-on-edge treatment into the box background while keeping text live.
rules.scope: email implementations, especially Outlook desktop/Word engine

rules.condition: When the pixel-perfect fallback is not feasible
rules.requirement: Ship the live version and accept the Outlook badge flush at the inner-left edge; retain Light Blue fill, 40px 8px 40px 8px box radius, blue weight-800 text, and badge shadow.
rules.scope: Outlook desktop only; other clients retain the absolute-positioned treatment

description: The palette-binding rule has null polarity even though the source explicitly binds each token to a component role. Its polarity and exact bindings should be explicit rather than unspecified.

The unconditional patient-marketing capitalization requirement for LISRAYA is not represented by either candidate rule. It is independent of the conditional trademark/generic-name rule and needs its own hard rule.

payload.requirement: Write LISRAYA in all caps.

description: This candidate combines an external-first-mention trademark rule with the generic-name formatting rule. The generic format applies whenever the generic name is used, while trademark placement is limited to the first/most prominent external-facing mention. In addition, the trademark source contains both positive and prohibitive obligations, which cannot be faithfully represented by a single must-polarity rule.

replacement_rules.scope: first or most prominent mention in external-facing documents when the generic name is included

replacement_rules.requirement: Do not use ® when the generic name is absent, and do not repeat ® on subsequent mentions.
Format the generic name as lowercase brepocitinib in parentheses: (brepocitinib).
replacement_rules.scope: whenever the generic name is used

description: A generic typography-table assembly conflates distinct rule families: channel/asset font assignments, permitted weights, email font loading/fallback, fixed email type-scale values, minimum-size constraints, line-height accessibility requirements, and hierarchy guidance. These have different scopes, hardness, and exception behavior and should not remain one broad binding.

split_rules.requirements: Headlines/headers use Agenda in print; Nunito Sans in digital/email; Aptos in Word/PPT/Excel. Body and footer/fine print use Nunito Sans in print/digital/email and Aptos in Office fallback.
Use the specified email font stack, type-scale values, minimum body/running-text sizes, and line-height requirements.
Use clear visual hierarchy; do not use adjacent same size-and-weight blocks to convey different hierarchy levels.

description: The second generic table-binding rule duplicates the same typography-hierarchy table source as the first candidate rule. There is no separate source partition supporting two parallel broad assembly rules.

The digital/email headline rule has a narrow conditional exception that must not be flattened into the default font assignment: Agenda may be live digital text only for the campaign headline. The default remains Nunito Sans with bolder weights and Title Case for digital/email headlines.

payload.requirement: Use Nunito Sans in bolder weights and Title Case for headlines and headers; Agenda is permitted as live text only when the text is the campaign headline.

description: Email accessibility/compliance constraints need explicit enforceable facets: Nunito Sans loading is conditional on client support while the fallback stack is mandatory; primary body copy has a mandated 16px minimum; running text must never be below 12px; and the ISI sizing exception is owned by a locked component rather than page authors.

governance.override_policy: No local override for ISI sizing

requirements: Set the CSS font stack to 'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif.
Load Nunito Sans using @font-face or a Google Fonts link only where supported; retain the full fallback stack for clients that strip webfonts.
Use at least 16px for primary body copy; use 14px supporting body sparingly and never for primary content; never set running text below 12px.
Use at least 150% line height for body copy and 160% for light text on dark backgrounds.
Do not author or override ISI text sizing; ISI sizing is owned by the locked component.

description: The source requires a distinct title-case rule for ordinary headlines/headings, including the specified lowercase treatment for articles, conjunctions, and prepositions. This requirement is not represented by the campaign-specific ALL-CAPS rule.

payload.requirement: Use initial caps/title case: capitalize nouns, pronouns, adjectives, verbs, and adverbs; lowercase articles, conjunctions, and prepositions.
payload.scope: headlines and headings other than explicitly defined ALL-CAPS exceptions

description: Chart/infographic headlines have an independent ALL-CAPS requirement and required font treatment. This is not covered by the named campaign-headline rule.

payload.requirement: Set headlines in ALL CAPS; use Agenda for print and Nunito Sans Bold or heavier for email fallback.

description: The body-copy sentence-case requirement is absent; it has a different content scope from the campaign headline rule and should be extracted separately.

payload.requirement: Use sentence case.

description: The source establishes an exception relationship: ALL CAPS is prohibited for body copy, buttons, and subheads except the enumerated cases, including the YOU DESERVE MORE campaign headline and chart/infographic headlines. The general prohibition and its exception links are missing.

payload.relation: The listed ALL-CAPS rules are explicit exceptions to this general prohibition.
payload.requirement: Do not use ALL CAPS.

description: No candidate rule captures the core bolding policy: its hierarchy-only purpose, per-section and per-paragraph limits, mandatory callout/benefit-claim uses, and explicit prohibitions including the approximately 15% body-text ceiling.

payload.rule: Use bold only for hierarchy and key takeaways, not decoration. Per email section, bold only headings, at most one key phrase or claim per paragraph, and the statistical-callout figure. Always bold required callout text, statistical-callout numbers, and the first focal brand-name instance in a benefit claim per section. Never bold whole paragraphs, footnotes/references, prepositional filler, generic names, or more than approximately 15% of running body text in a section.
payload.scope: email sections; paragraphs; callouts; benefit claims; running body text
payload.title: Bolding hierarchy, required uses, and limits

description: No candidate rule preserves the hard prohibition on combining bold, ALL CAPS, and color on one string, including its narrow chart-headline and campaign-lockup exception and the two-device maximum.

payload.rule: Do not apply bold, ALL CAPS, and color emphasis simultaneously to the same string. Exceptions are chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup. For every other element, use no more than two emphasis devices.
payload.scope: all emphasized strings; chart headlines; campaign headline lockups
payload.title: Limit stacked emphasis devices

description: No candidate rule captures the separate treatment of underlining and italics: underlining is limited to actual hyperlinks, while italics are prohibited as patient-copy emphasis and reserved for specified scholarly/note uses.

payload.rule: Use underlining only for real hyperlinks. Do not use italics for emphasis in patient copy; reserve italics for study names, notes such as 'Note:', and citations.
payload.scope: hyperlinks; patient copy; study names; notes; citations
payload.title: Restrict underlining and italics

description: The existing rule captures only the standalone-section first-mention condition. It omits the parent abbreviation policy: establish “dermatomyositis (DM)” on first mention, use DM thereafter in running copy, and prohibit DM in headlines, headings, and subheads. The standalone condition is an assembly-scoped qualification of that broader rule.

exceptions.condition: Assembly brief states that DM was established upstream
exceptions.effect: A standalone section need not re-establish the abbreviation.

scope.content: running copy, headlines, headings, subheads, standalone sections

payload.statement: Use the first-mention pattern “dermatomyositis (DM)”. After that first mention, use “DM” in running copy to avoid repetition. Never use “DM” in headlines, headings, or subheads; spell out “dermatomyositis” there. Every standalone section must independently establish “dermatomyositis (DM)” at its first mention unless the assembly brief explicitly states that the abbreviation was established upstream.

description: No candidate rule preserves the audience-governed inclusive-language requirements: “patients” is prohibited in patient materials but allowed in HCP materials, preferred person-first alternatives are required, and “care team” must replace “staff.” The patient-material scope and HCP exception are required governance facets.

payload.statement: In patient materials, use person-first language: “someone with dermatomyositis” or “people with dermatomyositis,” not “patients.” Use “care team,” not “staff,” when referring to the network of people with dermatomyositis, HCPs, and support systems.

description: The candidate group contains only the full-lockup copy rule and omits the distinct campaign-headline typography, casing, tinted-letter, colorway, and hero-layout requirements. These are independent hard rules and should be added as separate rules in the same campaign-headline group, not collapsed into the copy rule.

add_rule_specs.name: campaign_headline_font_and_channel_fallback
add_rule_specs.requirement: Set the Campaign Headline in Agenda. Digital may use Agenda as live text only for this headline; use Nunito Sans ExtraBold as the email fallback.

add_rule_specs.name: campaign_headline_all_caps
add_rule_specs.requirement: Render the Campaign Headline in all caps always.

add_rule_specs.name: campaign_headline_tinted_letters
add_rule_specs.requirement: Set the D in DESERVE and the M in MORE at approximately 40% tint of the headline color.

add_rule_specs.name: campaign_headline_approved_colorways
add_rule_specs.requirement: Use only Brand Gold/Sunshine or Brand Blue for the Campaign Headline; tinted letters must use 40% tint of that same hue.

add_rule_specs.name: campaign_headline_hero_stack_alignment
add_rule_specs.requirement: In hero placements, stack the headline as YOU / DESERVE / MORE on three lines and left-align it.

description: The explicit approval boundary for colorways is absent: the source permits exactly two approved colorways and requires tinted letters to remain the same hue. Treating this merely as a generic color preference would lose both the closed allowed set and the dependent same-hue constraint.

payload.dependent_requirement: D in DESERVE and M in MORE use 40% tint of the selected headline hue.

description: Channel and placement scopes are material qualifiers that must not be generalized: the Agenda live-text permission is digital-only and headline-only, the Nunito Sans ExtraBold fallback is email-only, and the three-line left-aligned layout applies only to hero placements.

scoped_requirements.requirement: Agenda may be used as live text.
Use Nunito Sans ExtraBold as the fallback.
Use a three-line YOU / DESERVE / MORE stack, left-aligned.

description: One assembly conflates a parent callout-formatting policy with three materially different approved callout variants, each with distinct visual specifications and responsive behavior. The global same-style noncompetition constraint should be retained as a parent/governance rule rather than merged into any variant.

rules.constraint: Never place two callouts of the same approved style in competition on one screen or section; use callouts judiciously.
rules.name: Callout formatting: approved styles and screen competition

responsive.two_column_icon_text_layout: collapse to single column on mobile

description: The candidate does not expose the governing facets in the heading: the approved style set, the screen/section scope, and the prohibition on two instances of the same style competing. These are not merely style attributes; they are a cross-callout cardinality/placement constraint.

A single strong_default rule with polarity None loses source polarity distinctions: “never two” is a hard prohibition, while the secondary callout’s line icon is explicitly optional (“may include one”). These requirements must not inherit one undifferentiated hardness or polarity.

The single hard-must rule conflates distinct campaign assets: headline, benefits copy, two alternative MOA formulations, administration copy, CTA, and support-program naming. These are not a single co-required message; in particular, the MOA entries are explicitly primary versus alt and should be selectable alternatives rather than jointly mandatory.

child_rules.scope: My Compass Support program naming and service details

description: The heading explicitly governs this material as an "Approved Campaign Messaging (verbatim framework)." The candidate exposes only hard/must and omits the approval and verbatim-use facets needed to prevent paraphrase, unapproved claim expansion, or use outside the approved framework. This is especially material for the FDA-approved, efficacy, steroid-reduction, and MOA claims.

The single core-principles rule conflates three independently testable voice dimensions with a separate contextual-flexibility/invariant rule. In particular, the confidence requirement is narrowly scoped to first-of-its-kind significance claims, while warmth and clarity govern different language qualities; contextual tone flexibility is permitted only insofar as the core voice remains unchanged.

rules.requirement: State first-of-its-kind significance clearly and directly; do not overstate or sensationalize it.
Use genuine empathy about DM's daily impact and keep patients at the center, without becoming overly emotional.
rules.scope: brand voice when discussing DM's daily impact and patients

rules.requirement: Use plain, respectful language that is easy to understand without sacrificing accuracy.
Tone may flex slightly by context, but the core brand voice must not change.

description: The clinical-claim requirement needs an explicit conditional scope (clinical claims only) and its cited governing reference (§1.2). The rule must also preserve the mandated † marker and the separated, noticeably smaller footnote treatment.

requirement.footnote_text: clearly separated from the claim and noticeably smaller

description: The candidate conflates two independently actionable requirements: quote attribution/styling and the required warm lifestyle-image pairing. Keeping it as an imagery-class rule obscures the copy-editorial attribution requirement; the two rules should be linked by a required-pairing relation.

rules.requirement: Pair the quote with warm lifestyle imagery

description: A must_not-only rule captures the prohibition on reproducing content in sections but loses the affirmative placement requirement: the listed legal and brand elements must live in locked header/footer components. The source contains both a positive location mandate and a negative section-scope prohibition.

The canvas/grid row conflates independent requirements with different scopes and strengths: fixed canvas sizing, responsive collapse behavior, advisory layout patterns, text-inset/full-bleed exception handling, and vertical-spacing tokens. A single strong-default row with null polarity weakens explicit mandatory requirements, including the 600px rendering requirement.

children.constraint: Email bodies must be fixed at 600px, centered, with assembled sections rendering edge-to-edge at exactly 600px.
All multi-column layouts must collapse to a single column at the 480px mobile breakpoint.
children.scope: email layouts at and below the 480px mobile breakpoint

children.constraint: Use a 12-column mental model; practical section layouts are one column, equal 50/50 two-column, or approximately 40/60 image-text splits.
Maintain 24px left and right section padding. Full-bleed imagery or gradient backgrounds may extend to 600px, but text inside them must retain the 24px inset.

Use an 8px base unit and make all padding and margins multiples of 8px. Each section owns 32px top and bottom internal padding; do not use external margins between sections. Use 16px heading-to-body and paragraph-to-paragraph gaps, 24px body-to-callout/CTA gaps, and 8px image-to-caption gaps.

description: The extracted CTA-cap rule omits the source's explicitly governed accessibility controls: contrast must be checked against the palette and clickable areas must be at least 48px under the stated brand accessibility rule. These need separately enforceable rules with an authority and verification facet, rather than being lost behind the per-section CTA-count rule.

rules.constraint: CTA clickable areas must be at least 48px high.
CTA foreground/background color combinations must be contrast-checked against the approved palette.

governance.verification: perform palette contrast check before approval

description: The CTA-count rule correctly captures the one-primary maximum, but the source also defines independently reusable primary and secondary CTA specifications. Those visual and secondary-variant requirements are unrepresented and should not be inferred from the count rule.

rules.constraint: Use Brand Blue #00529b background, White #ffffff text, Nunito Sans Bold 16px, 14px x 32px padding, and 24px fully rounded-pill corners.
Use a White background, 2px Brand Blue border, Brand Blue text, and the same type, padding, touch-target, and corner-radius metrics as the primary CTA.

description: No rule captures the required wave asset configuration: exactly two named colorways, each available in long and short variants.

payload.name: Wave colorways and variants
payload.requirement: Provide fluid wave forms only in the Sunshine/Gold and Sky Blue/Deep Blue colorways, with both long and short variants available for each colorway.

description: The mandatory wave-construction rule is absent. The source explicitly requires a 70% transparent top layer over a specified base layer and says the layered translucent quality must always be preserved.

payload.name: Wave layer construction
payload.requirement: Construct every wave with a top layer at 70% transparency over its base wave: Gold over Sunshine, or Sky Blue over Deep Blue. Always preserve the layered, translucent quality.

description: No governed usage rule records both the permitted wave placements and the color-control restriction. “Approved color values only” is a mandatory compliance constraint, while the listed placements and purposes are permitted uses rather than requirements.

payload.name: Wave usage and color approval
payload.permission: Waves may be used alone or overlaid on photography or gradient backgrounds to support layout flow, frame content, or add depth.
payload.requirement: Use approved color values only.

description: The approved masthead-copy device is missing, including its required gradient specification and logo-mark placement relation. The logo must be at the gradient’s lightest end.

payload.name: Approved masthead copy device
payload.requirement: Use the approved masthead gradient with logo mark: Sunshine to 0C,10M,78Y,0K, with the logo mark placed at the lightest end of the gradient.

description: The mandatory photography treatment is not represented: imagery must be warm, realistic patient lifestyle photography showing simple everyday activities and a return to everyday life.

payload.requirement: Use warm, realistic patient lifestyle imagery depicting real moments of simple everyday activity and conveying a return to everyday life / getting back to themselves.

description: The mandatory image-crop/container rule is missing. Images must use the Accent shape rather than plain rectangular or circular crops; the source also explicitly relates this rule to §2.5.

payload.requirement: House images in the Accent shape: asymmetric rounded-corner crops. Do not use plain rectangular or circular image crops.

description: The accessibility requirement has an explicit universal applicability condition in the source: every image requires descriptive alt text. That all-images scope should be encoded so the hard compliance rule is auditable and cannot be interpreted as applying only to selected image types.

payload.requirement: Every image requires descriptive alt text.

description: The candidate conflates the default asymmetric Brand Accent Shape system with a distinct charts-and-tables exception. The exception explicitly prohibits the asymmetric shape and therefore needs its own rule linked as an exception, rather than being represented by an assembly named "table_binding." The default system also contains separate print-token and email-implementation scopes that must remain explicit.

rules.requirement: Use a rectangular Brand Accent Shape with one diagonal pair of selectively enlarged corners and the opposite diagonal pair lightly rounded. Approved print radius pairs are 1.25in/0.0625in for large containers and photos, 0.5in/0.0625in for banners, and 0.4375in/0.0625in for small callout boxes. For email at 96dpi, use 120px/6px, 48px/6px, and 42px/6px respectively; use one consistent diagonal orientation across an email, defaulting to large radii at top-left and bottom-right.
rules.scope: general; callout text and photography containers

rules.requirement: Do not use the asymmetric Brand Accent Shape for charts or tables. Use a uniform 12px radius on all four corners.

description: The candidate's polarity is null despite source requirements containing both affirmative implementation instructions and an explicit prohibition: maintain one diagonal orientation consistently within an email, and do not use the asymmetric shape for charts/tables. Its apparent table-focused identity also reverses the source intent, because tables are governed by an exception rather than the core device.

relations.description: Charts and tables use uniform 12px rounding instead of the asymmetric accent shape.

payload.scope: general; accent-shape containers, with separate email implementation scope

description: The container specification and the bar/chart-icon geometry specification are independent concerns. The former includes panel/header color treatment and rounded container construction; the latter requires rounded, approachable bar tops. Combining them under one layout rule obscures distinct applicability and mixes color application with geometry.

requirements: Use a rounded Brand Blue header bar.
Use rounded, approachable shapes, including rounded bar tops.

description: This rule conflates three separately targeted typographic systems: channel-specific headlines, statistical callouts, and footnotes. In particular, the headline requirements have print/email conditional typography that cannot safely be governed by a generic data-and-footnote hierarchy rule.

requirements: Use Agenda for print headlines and Nunito Sans Bold+ for email headlines.
Set headlines in ALL CAPS and Brand Blue.
Place a thin Gold rule beneath the headline.
Make the percentage figure large and bold as the hero.
Place supporting copy beneath in a lighter weight.
Make footnotes noticeably smaller than primary data.
Clearly separate footnotes from primary data.

description: The candidate rule's data-and-footnote scope misses the headline-specific requirement, including the conditional relation of Agenda to print and Nunito Sans Bold+ to email. It also weakens categorical headline directives such as ALL CAPS, Brand Blue, and the thin Gold rule by representing the broader cluster as a strong-default/should rule.

payload.note: Remove headline coverage from this rule via split; represent headline treatment in a separate hard-constraint/must rule with print-versus-email conditional scope.

description: The candidate combines two independently scoped constraints: required visual construction for new icons and the palette-only restriction for icon colors/backgrounds. They have different governed attributes and should be separately enforceable.

child_rules.scope: New icons must use minimal line illustration with consistent stroke weight, rounded line caps, and single-color line work.
Icon colors and backgrounds may be adjusted only within the brand palette.

description: The group has no rule covering the separate low-vision accessibility guidance, including the email-specific 32×32px minimum render size and the default Brand Blue stroke treatment on light backgrounds. This is guidance/default behavior, not a hard universal requirement.

payload.scope: Consider scale and contrast for low-vision audiences; in email, use a minimum render size of 32×32px, with Brand Blue strokes as the default on light backgrounds.
