description: The candidate conflates two independently enforceable obligations with different actors and scopes: assembled-page background alternation and component-author metadata declaration. The latter is explicitly marked GENERAL and is a governance/authoring requirement, not merely an assembly-layout rule.

new_rules.requirement: Alternate section backgrounds using White, Light Blue, Golden Sand, or gradient backgrounds so adjacent assembled sections do not have identical backgrounds and do not visually merge.
Section designers must declare their section background color in component metadata so assemblers can sequence sections without identical adjacent backgrounds.
new_rules.scope: all section designers and component metadata

description: The explicit one-focal-point-per-section constraint is not represented. It is stronger and more testable than the surrounding general white-space guidance and should retain its per-section scope and contrast directive.

payload.guidance: Use generous white space and maintain a clean, never-cluttered layout.
payload.requirement: Limit each section to a single focal point, using contrast to direct attention to the most important element.

description: The source's show-don't-tell content/layout direction is absent. It should be captured as guidance rather than elevated to a hard requirement.

payload.requirement: Show rather than tell: break up text walls with imagery and icons.

description: The canonical content-block pattern and the all-materials reference-template mandate are unrepresented. The source establishes both a required block composition and a broad reference relationship governing visuals, messaging, and tone.

payload.requirement: Use the canonical content-block pattern of a bold headline, simple benefit statements, and a patient quote.

description: The approved color palette and its mandatory hierarchy are unrepresented: Deep Blue #00529B is dominant, Amber Orange #FAA31B is the secondary accent, and Golden Yellow #FFC60A is the tertiary accent.

payload.statement: Use only the approved brand colors in this hierarchy: #00529B Deep Blue as the dominant color, #FAA31B Amber Orange as the secondary accent, and #FFC60A Golden Yellow as the tertiary accent.

description: The headline typography requirement is missing: Agenda is the primary headline font and is required for all headlines and headers.

payload.statement: Use Agenda, the primary headline font, for all headlines and headers.

description: The print body-copy typography requirement is missing: Nunito Sans should be used for all body copy in print.

payload.statement: Use Nunito Sans for all body copy in print.

description: The CTA treatment requirement is missing. CTAs are directed to use the supplied asymmetric rounded shape and its left-to-right Golden Yellow (#FFDF55) to Amber Orange (#FAA21B) gradient treatment.

payload.statement: Use the supplied asymmetric rounded CTA shape and a horizontal gradient from #FFDF55 Golden Yellow on the left to #FAA21B Amber Orange on the right.

description: The brand-mandated accessibility minimum for body-copy size is absent. The hard 16px floor must not be weakened by the adjacent aspirational "bigger is better" guidance.

payload.text: Body copy must be at least 16px.

description: The nonmandatory preference to use body copy larger than the 16px minimum is missing and should remain a soft recommendation rather than being merged into the hard minimum.

relation.related_rule: Body copy must be at least 16px.

payload.text: Prefer body copy larger than 16px where possible.

description: The brand-mandated 150% minimum line-height requirement is absent.

payload.text: Line height must be at least 150%.

description: The contextual recommendation to use additional line height for light-on-dark text is missing; it is guidance beyond the hard 150% floor.

relation.related_rule: Line height must be at least 150%.

payload.text: Use more than 150% line height for light-on-dark text when needed for legibility.

description: The accessibility-directed requirement to use a type scale for visual hierarchy is absent.

payload.text: Use a type scale to establish visual hierarchy.

description: Contrast testing, the prohibition on color-only communication, and text-legibility proofing are independently testable obligations and must not be collapsed into one vague contrast rule.

rules.text: Never rely on color alone to communicate information.

description: Visible form labels and 48px click/tap targets are separate accessibility requirements; placeholder-label and disappearing-label bans must be retained as explicit negative constraints.

rules.text: Form labels must always remain visible; do not use placeholder text as a label or allow labels to disappear.
Click and tap targets must be at least 48px.

description: General animation behavior, pause controls, responsive-device coverage, and email-only motion constraints have different scopes and must be represented separately.

rules.text: Use quick animation transitions that do not interrupt flow, and avoid excessive motion.
Provide a way to pause effects.
Design for phone, tablet, and desktop.

description: The email-specific GIF first-frame requirement is absent. Its email-only scope and Outlook rationale must not be generalized to all animation.

payload.text: In email, animated GIFs must carry the key message in frame 1 because Outlook may show only the first frame.

description: The email-only restriction on autoplaying motion over approximately five seconds, including its static-fallback condition, is absent.

payload.text: In email, do not use autoplaying motion longer than approximately 5 seconds without a static fallback.

description: Image alt text and video/audio transcripts apply to different media types and should be separate mandatory accessibility rules.

rules.text: Provide descriptive alt text on all images.
Make transcripts available for every video or audio asset.

description: WCAG is named as the reference standard, but no rule records this external standard as governance/reference material. The wording does not itself establish a blanket WCAG-compliance mandate.

payload.text: Use WCAG (wcag.com) as the reference accessibility standard.

description: The candidate set omits the audience-governance rule: this guide governs patient/DTC materials, the audience must be confirmed in every brief before writing, and permitted terminology differs between patient and HCP materials.

payload.scope: Patient (DTC) materials; terminology exception for HCP materials
payload.statement: Confirm the target audience in every brief before writing. For patient/DTC materials, use "someone/people with dermatomyositis"; "patients" is acceptable in HCP materials.

description: The candidate combines distinct modular-assembly, section-metadata, footnote-assignment, locked-component adjacency, dark-section sequencing, and email-code compatibility requirements. These have different actors, scopes, timing, and polarity and should not be one rule.

rules.statement: Each section must be a self-contained 600px table-based block with its own 32px top and bottom padding and declared background color; sections are directly stacked with no outside margins.
Section metadata must declare background color, whether the section establishes dermatomyositis (DM), consumed footnote symbols, and whether the section contains a CTA.

Footnote symbols must be superscript and assigned in order of appearance at assembly time. Sections with more than one referenced claim must use placeholder tokens rather than hard-coded dagger symbols.

rules.scope: sections adjacent to locked header, footer, or ISI components
rules.statement: Sections adjacent to core-team-inserted locked components must not contain decorative elements that visually depend on an adjacent component.
A dark-background section must not be placed adjacent to another dark-background section.
Use HEX for all colors, px for all sizing, and inline CSS only; duplicate bgcolor attributes for Outlook compatibility.

description: Governance facets are missing: metadata is declared per section, footnote symbols are assigned at assembly time, locked components are inserted by the core team, and dark-background adjacency is explicitly an assembler responsibility. The declared metadata enables both footnote collision prevention and dark-section placement checks.

payload.governance_note: Apply section metadata before assembly; the assembler assigns footnote symbols and validates dark-background adjacency; the core team inserts locked header, footer, and ISI components.

description: A single must-polarity rule cannot faithfully represent the source's explicit prohibitions: no margins outside sections, no hard-coded daggers in multi-reference sections, no dependent decoration beside locked components, and no adjacent dark-background sections. These require separate must_not constraints.

payload.note: Retain must polarity only for the positive email-code compatibility rule from u_other_rules_2_0006; represent the source prohibitions as separate hard must_not rules.
